verbatim_text: And here are our results. So you can notice that by using the Bayesian system for both belief tracking, for either belief tracking or question selection, we get large improvements. And these improvements are also complementary. So both belief tracking and question selection benefit from explicit Bayesian modeling. OK, so also I've worked on some other works on addressing user modeling and under specification that didn't make it into this talk. But GATE was also one of the first papers to use language models for question asking, since inspired numerous follow ups focused on improving question asking and applying it across domains. This is also one of the major directions that industry is working on at scale, including the startup HumansAnd. OK, so this gives us one approach to improving state tracking, augmenting the AI with external user models by explicitly representing beliefs. The system can better infer preferences over turns. But equipping the AI with user models is only a first step towards building more collaborative AI agents. Beyond understanding humans, we also want a model of the AI. And in order to support this as AI developers, one thing we can do is to make AI more transparent about their own beliefs and reasoning.
